error_signature: Error in sub-node MCP Client Could not connect to your MCP server
title: MCP Client node connection error
symptom: The workflow fails with an error indicating that the MCP Client could not connect to the MCP server. This occurs during the execution of the node.
not_this_if: Using a single web instance instead of multiple
MCP server is reachable from the network
root_cause: The error occurs when the MCP Client node cannot establish a connection to the specified MCP server endpoint. This can be due to network issues, incorrect endpoint configuration, or the workflow not being active.
fix_steps: Ensure the MCP server is running and accessible from the n8n instance.
Verify that the endpoint URL in the MCP Client node is correct.
Check that the workflow containing the MCP Client node is active.
agent_instruction: Verify the MCP server's accessibility and ensure the workflow is active.
